title: theodorea obvallata
intro: Theodorea obvallata, also known as the False Sandalwood or Bubinga, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Theodorea obvallata include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of Theodorea obvallata include decoctions, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
